meta_title: Impact Doors in Inverrary, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Inverrary, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your home today.
h1: Impact Doors for Inverrary Homes — Built to Withstand South Florida's Worst
intro:
Living in Inverrary means enjoying the charm of one of Broward County's most beloved residential communities — the tree-lined streets of Inverrary Country Club, the close-knit feel of Inverrary Village, and the kind of neighborhood pride that keeps home values steady around $280,000. But it also means living with the undeniable reality of South Florida's hurricane season, and that reality demands more than wishful thinking when it comes to protecting your front door, back patio, or French doors from a major storm. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout the region as 'The Kings of Service,' is here to make sure your home is ready for whatever the Atlantic throws your way.

Inverrary sits in Broward County's interior, approximately seven miles from the coastline, but don't let that distance lull you into a false sense of security. The area falls within a 170 mph wind design zone, meaning that even without direct coastal exposure, homes here face extraordinary wind loads during major hurricanes. The storms that have swept through this zip code — 33319 — over the decades have left a clear message: standard doors are not enough. Impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors with multi-point locking systems aren't a luxury upgrade in Inverrary; they're a structural necessity that every homeowner should take seriously.

With roughly half of Inverrary's housing stock built before 1994 — before modern Florida Building Code standards reshaped hurricane construction requirements — tens of thousands of square feet of door openings across this community are still protected by older, single-point-lock doors that were never engineered for today's wind load standards. If your home in Inverrary Country Club or Inverrary Village was built in the 1970s or 1980s, the doors that came with it likely aren't up to current code and almost certainly won't qualify for maximum wind mitigation credits on your homeowner's insurance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing specializes in bringing those older homes up to modern standards efficiently, affordably, and with the kind of craftsmanship that Inverrary homeowners have come to trust.

section_why.h2: Why Inverrary Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When Inverrary residents decide it's time to upgrade to impact doors, they quickly discover that not all installers are created equal. Many contractors in Broward County subcontract their labor — meaning the crew that shows up at your home may have never worked with the specific door brand or product line being installed. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we do things differently. Every installation is performed 100% in-house by our own trained, licensed, and insured technicians. No subcontractors. No strangers on your property who don't represent our values. Just our people, doing the work the right way, every single time.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience in impact door installation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. That means we've seen virtually every home configuration, structural challenge, and permit scenario that Inverrary homes can present — including the quirks of older construction common in the Inverrary Country Club and Inverrary Village neighborhoods. We know how to properly frame around legacy doorways, how to ensure airtight seals on sliding glass door tracks, and how to make sure every French door installation meets Broward County Building Division requirements without delay.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 homes across South Florida and holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau — not because we say the right things, but because we consistently deliver results that exceed expectations. Our customers in neighboring cities like Lauderhill, Tamarac, and Sunrise have spread the word, and Inverrary homeowners are increasingly turning to us as the region's most trusted impact door specialist. We pull all required permits, coordinate directly with the Broward County Building Division, and handle every inspection milestone so you never have to chase down a subcontractor or wonder whether your installation was done correctly.

From your initial consultation through final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire process. We bring product samples to your home, explain your options clearly, and never pressure you into a decision before you're ready. That transparent, respectful approach — combined with in-house expertise and premium product brands — is exactly why Inverrary homeowners call us 'The Kings of Service.'

section_history.h2: Storm History That Makes Impact Doors Essential in Inverrary
section_history.body:
Inverrary's storm history is a sobering reminder that Broward County's interior communities are far from immune to hurricane devastation. Since 1990, the area has experienced nine named storms of significance — but three stand out as defining events for local homeowners. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a catastrophic wake-up call for all of South Florida, obliterating thousands of homes and exposing how dramatically underbuilt the region's housing stock was for major wind events. While Andrew's core destruction was centered in Miami-Dade, Broward County communities like Inverrary sustained serious wind damage that prompted the sweeping building code reforms now known as the modern Florida Building Code.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 struck closer to home for Inverrary residents, tracking directly across Broward County as a powerful Category 3 system. Wilma's damage was widespread and particularly cruel to homes with older entry doors and sliding glass doors — those weak points gave way under sustained winds, allowing rain-driven water to pour inside and compound structural damage significantly. Homeowners who had already upgraded to impact-rated doors reported dramatically less interior damage, reinforcing what insurance adjusters and building inspectors had been saying for years. Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as another stress test, bringing sustained tropical-storm-to-hurricane-force winds across the county and once again exposing vulnerabilities in non-impact door systems.

The pattern across all nine storms is consistent: the door openings of a home — entry doors, sliding glass doors, French doors — are among the most vulnerable points of failure during a major wind event. Once a door fails and the building envelope is breached, interior pressurization can cause catastrophic roof and wall failures that dwarf the original door damage. For the pre-1994 homes that make up roughly half of Inverrary's housing stock, this risk is especially pronounced. Upgrading to properly rated impact doors isn't just about surviving the next storm — it's about ensuring your family and your $280,000 investment remain protected through every storm season to come.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Broward County
section_code.body:
Inverrary falls within Broward County's jurisdiction, and while it is not located within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation typically reserved for Miami-Dade and parts of coastal Broward — the community is still governed by stringent Florida Building Code requirements that mandate hurricane-rated products for all exterior door openings. This distinction matters because some homeowners mistakenly believe that being outside the HVHZ means standard doors are acceptable. They are not. Broward County requires that all exterior doors, including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, meet the wind load requirements for the specific design wind speed of the installation location — which in Inverrary's zip code 33319 is a demanding 170 mph.

What this means practically is that any replacement door installed in an Inverrary home must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Product Approval demonstrating it has been tested and rated for the applicable wind load and impact resistance requirements under the Florida Building Code. Products that carry Miami-Dade County NOAs are generally recognized as meeting or exceeding Broward County's standards as well, which is why many of the products Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs in Inverrary carry that prestigious designation. All work must be permitted through the Broward County Building Division, and installations must pass a final inspection before the permit can be closed — something Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles on your behalf from start to finish.

For pre-1994 homes, the permitting process may also involve a review of existing framing and rough openings to ensure they can properly support modern impact door systems. Our experienced project managers are well-versed in these requirements and routinely coordinate with Broward County inspectors to ensure smooth approvals. We never skip the permit process or encourage homeowners to do so — unpermitted work not only creates legal liability but can void your homeowner's insurance coverage and complicate future home sales. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, every impact door installation in Inverrary is done completely by the book.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Inverrary Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door product lines in the industry — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI — both of which are purpose-built to perform under the extreme wind load conditions that Inverrary homes must be designed to withstand. These aren't off-the-shelf products pulled from a big-box retailer; they are professionally engineered, rigorously tested, and manufactured specifically for the demanding requirements of South Florida's hurricane-prone environment.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) impact doors are engineered with heavy-duty aluminum or steel frames paired with laminated safety glass that holds together even when struck by wind-borne debris traveling at hurricane speeds. Their multi-point locking systems are a critical feature — rather than relying on a single latch point as traditional doors do, ESW doors engage locking mechanisms at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously, dramatically increasing resistance to wind pressure and forced entry. This makes them an excellent choice for both front entry doors and double French door configurations common in Inverrary Country Club homes. CWI impact doors complement the ESW lineup with their own high-performance sliding glass door systems, which are particularly well-suited to the rear patio and lanai configurations typical of Inverrary Village residences.

Both ESW and CWI product lines are available in a wide range of finishes, glass options, and hardware styles — meaning Inverrary homeowners don't have to sacrifice curb appeal for protection. Whether you're looking for a bold mahogany-style entry door that enhances your home's streetfront presence or a low-profile sliding glass door that keeps your backyard view unobstructed, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's product selection can accommodate your aesthetic preferences while delivering the structural performance your home demands. Both brands carry the required product approvals for installation under Broward County's permitting requirements, giving you confidence that your investment is fully compliant and fully protected.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Inverrary Homeowners
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Inverrary homeowners is: 'What is this going to cost me?' It's a fair question, and we believe in giving honest, transparent answers. The cost of impact door installation in Inverrary varies based on the number of door openings, the size of each opening, the product configuration (entry door, sliding glass door, or French door), and the complexity of the existing rough opening. For a typical Inverrary home valued around $280,000, a single impact entry door installation might range from approximately $2,500 to $5,500 fully installed, while a large sliding glass door system could range from $4,000 to $8,000 or more depending on size and specifications. Whole-home door packages covering entry, side, and patio doors can offer economies of scale and often represent the best overall value.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes these investments accessible for middle-income Inverrary households through flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down required for qualified applicants. We work with financing partners who understand the home improvement landscape in South Florida and can tailor repayment terms to fit your budget without compromising on the quality of the products or installation. When you factor in the long-term return on investment — including reduced risk of catastrophic storm damage, lower homeowner's insurance premiums, and improved energy efficiency — impact door installation often pays for itself faster than homeowners expect.

From a pure home value perspective, impact-rated doors are increasingly a standard expectation among buyers in the Inverrary real estate market. Homes without them can face buyer hesitation, lower appraisals, or requests for seller concessions. By investing in impact doors today, you're not just protecting your family — you're protecting and potentially enhancing the resale value of one of your most significant financial assets. Our team is happy to walk you through a full cost-benefit analysis during your no-obligation consultation.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Benefits for Inverrary Residents
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance costs in Broward County have climbed sharply in recent years, driven by storm losses, rising reinsurance costs, and insurer departures from the Florida market. For Inverrary residents, impact doors represent one of the most direct ways to push back against rising premiums. Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer discounts for homes with hurricane-rated opening protections, and impact doors — when properly documented through a wind mitigation inspection — qualify for meaningful premium reductions that can add up to hundreds of dollars per year.

The wind mitigation credit structure under Florida's rating system rewards homes that protect all openings — not just windows. A home that has impact windows but still has standard, non-rated doors may not qualify for the maximum opening protection credit, leaving money on the table every single year. By completing your home's opening protection with ESW and CWI impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you position yourself to receive the full suite of available discounts from carriers like Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and other carriers active in the Broward County market. We always recommend that homeowners schedule a licensed wind mitigation inspector after their installation to formally document the upgrades and submit updated credits to their carrier.

Beyond direct premium discounts, impact doors can also reduce your policy's deductible exposure during a named storm event. Many policies in Florida carry separate hurricane deductibles — often 2% to 5% of the insured home value — that kick in during declared disasters. On a $280,000 Inverrary home, that could mean a $5,600 to $14,000 out-of-pocket exposure during a major storm. Impact doors that prevent a breach of the building envelope can significantly reduce actual storm damage, meaning you may never need to trigger that deductible at all. That's protection that has a real, calculable financial value beyond any insurance premium savings alone.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Inverrary if I'm not on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Inverrary sits in a 170 mph wind design zone under the Florida Building Code, and being seven miles from the coast doesn't meaningfully reduce wind exposure during a major hurricane. Storms like Wilma in 2005 and Irma in 2017 caused significant door and window failures throughout Inverrary's interior neighborhoods. Impact doors protect your home's building envelope from wind pressure and debris — one of the most critical factors in preventing catastrophic interior damage during any hurricane.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in Inverrary, and what are my financing options?
faqs.a: Costs vary by door type and configuration, but most Inverrary homeowners can expect to invest between $2,500 and $8,000 per door opening, fully installed. Whole-home packages covering multiple doors often provide the best value per opening. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants, making it manageable for middle-income households. We provide detailed, transparent quotes with no surprise fees so you know exactly what you're committing to before any work begins.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Broward County, and how does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for impact door replacements in Broward County, regardless of whether you're in the HVHZ. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from preparing and submitting the application to the Broward County Building Division, to coordinating all required inspections, to securing the final sign-off. We never encourage homeowners to skip permits, as unpermitted work can void insurance coverage and create complications during future home sales.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Inverrary?
faqs.a: Most single-door installations can be completed in a single day by our in-house crew. Whole-home projects involving multiple entry, patio, and French door openings typically take one to three days depending on scope and existing framing conditions. Permit approval timelines with Broward County Building Division vary but generally range from two to four weeks. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages all scheduling and will keep you informed at every milestone so you always know where your project stands.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact doors in Inverrary?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and policy, but Broward County homeowners commonly see annual premium reductions of 10% to 30% after completing their home's opening protection with impact-rated doors and windows and submitting updated wind mitigation documentation. On a typical Inverrary policy, that can translate to hundreds of dollars in annual savings. We strongly recommend scheduling a licensed wind mitigation inspection after installation and notifying your insurance carrier promptly to begin capturing those credits as quickly as possible.
